public_links.title: Wikipedia: Thermoelectric Generator Principles and Seebeck Effect
Appropedia: Biomass Cookstove Thermoelectric Generator Research

technical_documents.description: Clamps Peltier modules to the side of any cookstove to convert waste heat into 5V USB electricity while simultaneously cooking meals.
technical_documents.problem_statement: 1.2 billion people cook on open biomass fires daily, with 80% of thermal energy lost as waste heat while their phones remain uncharged.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: Four TEG-12706 Peltier modules are clamped between an aluminium heat collector plate and a water-cooled cold block, generating 8–15W of 5V USB power during a typical 2-hour cooking session.

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Cut aluminium plate to match cookstove side dimensions; grind surface flat for good thermal contact.
Step 2: Apply thermal paste to hot side of each Peltier module and press onto collector plate.
Step 3: Attach water-cooled cold block to the cold side with spring clamps applying 50N pressure.
Step 4: Wire all four modules in series-parallel configuration to the USB regulator input.
Step 5: Connect cold block hoses to a gravity-fed water reservoir; place on stove rim during cooking.

comments.text: Genius! I get 10W consistently on my charcoal jiko. Charges a phone to 60% per meal.
Any suggestions for thermal paste alternatives available in rural areas?
